notes: 1962 NCB borehole, typed log, 3 pages. Document heading: 'Section of Borehole near Club Lane, Whitesmocks — Bearpark Colliery'; purpose 'To prove the Harvey Seam (N) and Tilley Seam (P)'; exact site NZ 25706 43348 (differs slightly from the BGS index grid ref 425703,543341); commenced 7/8/62, completed 24/9/62, N.C.B. No. 1 Rig; 6-inch map Dm. 19 S.E., B/H Regd No. 55, Nat Grid box NZ 24 S.E. 31. Columns are THICKNESS (feet|in) and DEPTH (feet|in) — no fathoms column; _ffi arrays therefore carry fathoms=0. The thickness column is populated ONLY for the Old Workings (2-10), Harvey coal (1-10) and the three Tilley rows (0-7, 0-7, 0-1); all other thickness cells are blank in the original (recorded [0,0,0]) — derive thicknesses from successive depths downstream. Open hole 0-380 ft (drift descriptions coarse), 'Cored from 380'' noted in the log; detailed core descriptions below. One clerk arithmetic slip at the Harvey Seam (interval 32): 414-7 + 1-10 = 416-5 but depth written 416-6; a faint typed mark after 414-7 (interval 31) plausibly reads '½', which would nearly reconcile it. Typewriter has an ink-clogged '0' slug (inches cells at 160 ft and 457 ft read as bold blobs). Header pencil arithmetic by a later hand: '416.50 / 323.69 / 92.81' (Harvey base 416'6" = 416.5 ft below a surface of +323.69 ft OD puts the seam at c.-92.81 ft OD), '324 - 240 = 84' (rockhead elevation), '66' (Hutton elevation); pencil in the classification column: 'BCl - 240 / RH 84 / HU - 66'. No water observations recorded anywhere on the log. Final depth 469 ft 0 in = 142.95 m, matching the BGS index depth 142.95 m.
